questions.question: Explain the three fundamental transformations in the synthetic camera model with their purpose.
keyPoints: Model transformation: transforms object coordinates to world coordinates
View transformation: transforms world coordinates to camera coordinates
Projection transformation: transforms camera coordinates to 2D image coordinates

questions.question: Define the viewing frustum and explain its components.
keyPoints: Viewing frustum is the 3D volume visible to the camera
It has 6 clipping planes: near, far, left, right, top, bottom
In perspective, it's a truncated pyramid; in orthographic, it's a rectangular box

questions.question: Differentiate between perspective and orthographic projection.
keyPoints: Perspective: parallel lines converge at vanishing points, distance affects size
Orthographic: parallel lines remain parallel, size unaffected by distance
Perspective simulates human vision, orthographic used in technical drawings

questions.question: What is the purpose of near and far clipping planes in the synthetic camera model?
keyPoints: Near plane: prevents division by zero, clips objects too close to camera
Far plane: clips objects beyond this distance
Together they define the depth range of the view volume

questions.question: List and explain the camera parameters required to define a synthetic camera.
keyPoints: Camera position (eye point) in world coordinates
Look-at point (where camera is viewing)
Up vector (defines camera's upward direction
Field of View (angular extent of visible scene)
Aspect ratio (width/height of image)

questions.question: Explain the complete graphics rendering pipeline with emphasis on the role of synthetic camera model.
keyPoints: Object coordinates → Model Matrix → World coordinates
World coordinates → View Matrix → Camera/Eye coordinates
Camera coordinates → Projection Matrix → Clip coordinates
Clip coordinates → Perspective Division → NDC
NDC → Viewport Transform → Screen coordinates
Role of synthetic camera: defines view volume, clipping planes, projection type

questions.question: Derive or explain the perspective projection transformation matrix and explain perspective divide.
keyPoints: Perspective matrix creates foreshortening effect
Points are represented in homogeneous coordinates (x,y,z,w)
Projection matrix sets w' = -z (distance from camera)
Perspective divide: (x/w', y/w', z/w') converts 3D to 2D
This causes distant objects to appear smaller
